newsroom@santacruzsentinel.com.cold_pitch_body:
Hi Santacruzsentinel team,

(Writing to the Santacruzsentinel newsroom rather than Pat directly — we couldn't locate a direct contact. Feel free to forward if it's a better fit for them or the desk.)

Loved seeing your FAIR Plan reporting picked up in the Sentinel with the Altadena imagery. Pairing the rate-hike numbers with that specific street made the abstraction land in a way pure policy coverage rarely does.

A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista. In Europe, resilience has been default construction practice for generations.

One thought your piece sparked: what California is now codifying through WUI rules and pricing into FAIR Plan premiums has been normal Mediterranean practice since long before insurability became a topic here. That cultural lag feels like the interesting story.

Would you be interested in receiving more material, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la

A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista.

One thought your piece sparked: what the US custom market is now discovering through ICF has been default Mediterranean practice for generations, just expressed differently. The cultural translation is, we think, the interesting story.

Would you be interested in receiving more material from our side, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team

touches.subject: A European angle on the Washington IBHS program

editor@insurancenewsnet.com.source_title: Wildfire designation program aims to help Washington homeowners keep insurance - Insurance News | InsuranceNewsNet
editor@insurancenewsnet.com.cold_pitch_body:
Hi Insurance News Net team,

(Writing to the Insurance News Net newsroom directly — this is meant for whichever desk is closest to the topic below. Feel free to forward as appropriate.)

Really appreciated the piece on Washington's IBHS Wildfire Prepared Home rollout, particularly the detail on the three-year designation cycle and annual photo verification. That mitigation is treated as an ongoing relationship with the carrier, not a one-time stamp, is the part most coverage misses.

A quick hello from our side: at My Villa, we bring European construction resilience and the Italian way of living to homes in Los Angeles, designed in exposed reinforced concrete, cemento a vista.

One thought your piece sparked: what IBHS is now formalizing as a maintained designation has been default Mediterranean construction practice for generations, built in from day one rather than verified annually. The cultural gap feels like the real story.

Would you be interested in receiving more material on what we are doing, or in setting up a short call with our founder Paolo?

Best,
Lisa Monelli
My Villa Media Team
info@myvilla.la · myvilla.la
